## Commitments by V (Vrijen Attawar)

| # | Commitment | Context | Urgency |
|---|-----------|---------|---------|
| 1 | **Send Emre the recording of the Zo event talk** | V referenced a talk he did with the Zo team about people lacking primitives for transitioning from deterministic to stochastic mental models: *"I'm happy to send you the recording of this talk that we did"* | Near-term follow-up |
| 2 | **Send Emre the survey results from the event** | N=27 respondents out of 80-90 attendees; data showing 50% used Claude Code/agentic tools, 100% used ChatGPT, ~80% felt overwhelmed: *"I'll send you these survey results that I got from the event that we held"* | Near-term follow-up |
| 3 | **Send Emre the notes** | Referenced alongside the recording: *"I'll send you the notes in a second as well"* | Near-term follow-up |

## Implicit Follow-ups (Not Explicitly Committed)

- **Explore shared ground on "cognitive scaffolding" / AI coaching thesis** — Both V and Emre independently arrived at the idea that people need guided help adopting AI, not just tools. No formal next step was set, but the alignment is strong enough to warrant a follow-up.
- **Emre to evaluate Zo as a reference point** — V demo'd his meeting processing pipeline on Zo; Emre showed genuine curiosity about the platform's approach (*"sounds like Zo is kind of like Obsidian but for a different kind of system with AI automation in it"*).

## No Deadlines Were Set

Neither party established specific timelines. All commitments are soft follow-ups from a first introductory conversation.
